summary: Doctor-oriented professional admin interface with high-tech smart UI, glass effects, and dynamic section visibility (sections hidden until content is added).
architectural_notes: UI theme: high-tech, doctor-oriented, glass morphism effects, professional admin interface
Sections should be conditionally rendered/visible only when content exists

new_feature_requests.summary: Hide empty sections until content is added
Professional doctor-oriented smart UI with glass effects and powerful admin interface

Hide any public-facing section (About, Clinics, Services, Social Media) entirely when it has no content. A section is considered empty if it has no items (for list-based sections) or no meaningful text/image (for single-content sections). Empty sections must be invisible to visitors — no placeholder, no skeleton, no empty card grid.

Redesign the overall public-facing UI and admin interface with a professional, doctor-oriented aesthetic. Apply a clean medical-grade design system: crisp white and deep charcoal tones with a trusted clinical accent (e.g., teal or soft emerald), refined typography using Space Grotesk for headings and Inter for body text, precise spacing, and subtle micro-interactions. The design must convey authority, precision, and trust — appropriate for a medical professional's digital presence.

Apply glassmorphism (frosted glass) effects consistently across cards, modals, navigation, and panel surfaces throughout both the public site and the admin dashboard. Glass panels should use semi-transparent backgrounds with backdrop blur, a subtle light border, and a soft inner glow. Ensure legibility of text on all glass surfaces in both light and dark contexts.

Redesign the AdminDashboard into a powerful, smart admin interface. The sidebar should be structured with clear section groupings and icons, display active state clearly, and feel like a professional CMS control panel. The content area should use card-based editors with clear visual hierarchy, inline status indicators (saved, unsaved changes), and section health indicators showing which sections are currently empty vs. populated.
